root_text.an10.99:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā upāli yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā upāli bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.99:2.1: “Durabhisambhavāni hi kho, upāli, araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni.
root_text.an10.99:2.3: Ekatte haranti maññe mano vanāni samādhiṁ alabhamānassa bhikkhuno.
root_text.an10.99:2.5: ‘ahaṁ samādhiṁ alabhamāno araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevissāmī’ti, tassetaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an10.99:3.2: Atha āgaccheyya hatthināgo sattaratano vā aḍḍhaṭṭharatano vā.
root_text.an10.99:3.4: ‘yannūnāhaṁ imaṁ udakarahadaṁ ogāhetvā kaṇṇasandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷeyyaṁ piṭṭhisandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷeyyaṁ.
root_text.an10.99:3.5: Kaṇṇasandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷitvā piṭṭhisandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷitvā nhatvā ca pivitvā ca paccuttaritvā yena kāmaṁ pakkameyyan’ti.
root_text.an10.99:3.6: So taṁ udakarahadaṁ ogāhetvā kaṇṇasandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷeyya piṭṭhisandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷeyya;
root_text.an10.99:3.7: kaṇṇasandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷitvā piṭṭhisandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷitvā nhatvā ca pivitvā ca paccuttaritvā yena kāmaṁ pakkameyya.
root_text.an10.99:3.9: Mahā, upāli, attabhāvo gambhīre gādhaṁ vindati.
root_text.an10.99:4.1: Atha āgaccheyya saso vā biḷāro vā.
root_text.an10.99:4.4: Yannūnāhaṁ imaṁ udakarahadaṁ ogāhetvā kaṇṇasandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷeyyaṁ piṭṭhisandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷeyyaṁ;
root_text.an10.99:4.5: kaṇṇasandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷitvā piṭṭhisandhovikampi khiḍḍaṁ kīḷitvā nhatvā ca pivitvā ca paccuttaritvā yena kāmaṁ pakkameyyan’ti.
root_text.an10.99:4.6: So taṁ udakarahadaṁ sahasā appaṭisaṅkhā pakkhandeyya.
root_text.an10.99:4.10: Paritto, upāli, attabhāvo gambhīre gādhaṁ na vindati.
root_text.an10.99:4.11: Evamevaṁ kho, upāli, yo evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an10.99:4.12: ‘ahaṁ samādhiṁ alabhamāno araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevissāmī’ti, tassetaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an10.99:5.1: Seyyathāpi, upāli, daharo kumāro mando uttānaseyyako sakena muttakarīsena kīḷati.
root_text.an10.99:6.1: “Sa kho so, upāli, kumāro aparena samayena vuddhimanvāya indriyānaṁ paripākamanvāya
root_text.an10.99:6.2: yāni kānici kumārakānaṁ kīḷāpanakāni bhavanti, seyyathidaṁ—vaṅkakaṁ ghaṭikaṁ mokkhacikaṁ ciṅgulakaṁ pattāḷhakaṁ rathakaṁ dhanukaṁ, tehi kīḷati.
root_text.an10.99:6.4: nanvāyaṁ khiḍḍā purimāya khiḍḍāya abhikkantatarā ca paṇītatarā cā”ti?
root_text.an10.99:7.1: “Sa kho so, upāli, kumāro aparena samayena vuddhimanvāya indriyānaṁ paripākamanvāya
root_text.an10.99:7.3: cakkhuviññeyyehi rūpehi iṭṭhehi kantehi manāpehi piyarūpehi kāmūpasaṁhitehi rajanīyehi,
root_text.an10.99:7.7: kāyaviññeyyehi phoṭṭhabbehi iṭṭhehi kantehi manāpehi piyarūpehi kāmūpasaṁhitehi rajanīyehi.
root_text.an10.99:7.9: nanvāyaṁ khiḍḍā purimāhi khiḍḍāhi abhikkantatarā ca paṇītatarā cā”ti?
root_text.an10.99:8.1: “Idha kho pana vo, upāli, tathāgato loke uppajjati arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā.
root_text.an10.99:8.2: So imaṁ lokaṁ sadevakaṁ samārakaṁ sabrahmakaṁ sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiṁ pajaṁ sadevamanussaṁ sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā pavedeti.
root_text.an10.99:8.3: So dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāseti.
root_text.an10.99:9.1: Taṁ dhammaṁ suṇāti gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā aññatarasmiṁ vā kule paccājāto.
root_text.an10.99:9.2: So taṁ dhammaṁ sutvā tathāgate saddhaṁ paṭilabhati.
root_text.an10.99:9.3: So tena saddhāpaṭilābhena samannāgato iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an10.99:9.5: Nayidaṁ sukaraṁ agāraṁ ajjhāvasatā ekantaparipuṇṇaṁ ekantaparisuddhaṁ saṅkhalikhitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carituṁ.
root_text.an10.99:9.6: Yannūnāhaṁ kesamassuṁ ohāretvā kāsāyāni vatthāni acchādetvā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajeyyan’ti.
root_text.an10.99:10.1: So aparena samayena appaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ pahāya mahantaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ pahāya appaṁ vā ñātiparivaṭṭaṁ pahāya mahantaṁ vā ñātiparivaṭṭaṁ pahāya kesamassuṁ ohāretvā kāsāyāni vatthāni acchādetvā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajati.
root_text.an10.99:11.1: So evaṁ pabbajito samāno bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāsājīvasamāpanno pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti nihitadaṇḍo nihitasattho lajjī dayāpanno sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī viharati.
root_text.an10.99:12.1: Adinnādānaṁ pahāya adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti dinnādāyī dinnapāṭikaṅkhī; athenena sucibhūtena attanā viharati.
root_text.an10.99:13.1: Abrahmacariyaṁ pahāya brahmacārī hoti ārācārī virato methunā gāmadhammā.
root_text.an10.99:14.1: Musāvādaṁ pahāya musāvādā paṭivirato hoti saccavādī saccasandho theto paccayiko avisaṁvādako lokassa.
root_text.an10.99:15.1: Pisuṇaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pisuṇāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti, ito sutvā na amutra akkhātā imesaṁ bhedāya, amutra vā sutvā na imesaṁ akkhātā amūsaṁ bhedāya. Iti bhinnānaṁ vā sandhātā sahitānaṁ vā anuppadātā, samaggārāmo samaggarato samagganandī; samaggakaraṇiṁ vācaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an10.99:16.1: Pharusaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti. Yā sā vācā nelā kaṇṇasukhā pemanīyā hadayaṅgamā porī bahujanakantā bahujanamanāpā, tathārūpiṁ vācaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an10.99:17.1: Samphappalāpaṁ pahāya samphappalāpā paṭivirato hoti kālavādī bhūtavādī atthavādī dhammavādī vinayavādī, nidhānavatiṁ vācaṁ bhāsitā hoti kālena sāpadesaṁ pariyantavatiṁ atthasaṁhitaṁ.
root_text.an10.99:19.1: So santuṭṭho hoti kāyaparihārikena cīvarena kucchiparihārikena piṇḍapātena.
root_text.an10.99:19.3: seyyathāpi nāma pakkhī sakuṇo yena yeneva ḍeti sapattabhārova ḍeti.
root_text.an10.99:19.4: Evamevaṁ bhikkhu santuṭṭho hoti kāyaparihārikena cīvarena kucchiparihārikena piṇḍapātena. Yena yeneva pakkamati samādāyeva pakkamati.
root_text.an10.99:19.5: So iminā ariyena sīlakkhandhena samannāgato ajjhattaṁ anavajjasukhaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an10.99:20.1: So cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī.
root_text.an10.99:20.2: Yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ cakkhundriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati; rakkhati cakkhundriyaṁ, cakkhundriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an10.99:20.7: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī.
root_text.an10.99:20.8: Yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ manindriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati; rakkhati manindriyaṁ, manindriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an10.99:20.9: So iminā ariyena indriyasaṁvarena samannāgato ajjhattaṁ abyāsekasukhaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an10.99:21.1: So abhikkante paṭikkante sampajānakārī hoti, ālokite vilokite sampajānakārī hoti, samiñjite pasārite sampajānakārī hoti, saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇe sampajānakārī hoti, asite pīte khāyite sāyite sampajānakārī hoti, uccārapassāvakamme sampajānakārī hoti, gate ṭhite nisinne sutte jāgarite bhāsite tuṇhībhāve sampajānakārī hoti.
root_text.an10.99:22.1: So iminā ca ariyena sīlakkhandhena samannāgato, iminā ca ariyena indriyasaṁvarena samannāgato, iminā ca ariyena satisampajaññena samannāgato
root_text.an10.99:22.2: vivittaṁ senāsanaṁ bhajati araññaṁ rukkhamūlaṁ pabbataṁ kandaraṁ giriguhaṁ susānaṁ vanapatthaṁ abbhokāsaṁ palālapuñjaṁ.
root_text.an10.99:22.3: So araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.an10.99:23.1: So abhijjhaṁ loke pahāya vigatābhijjhena cetasā viharati, abhijjhāya cittaṁ parisodheti.
root_text.an10.99:23.2: Byāpādapadosaṁ pahāya abyāpannacitto viharati sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī, byāpādapadosā cittaṁ parisodheti.
root_text.an10.99:23.3: Thinamiddhaṁ pahāya vigatathinamiddho viharati ālokasaññī sato sampajāno, thinamiddhā cittaṁ parisodheti.
root_text.an10.99:23.4: Uddhaccakukkuccaṁ pahāya anuddhato viharati ajjhattaṁ vūpasantacitto, uddhaccakukkuccā cittaṁ parisodheti.
root_text.an10.99:23.5: Vicikicchaṁ pahāya tiṇṇavicikiccho viharati akathaṅkathī kusalesu dhammesu, vicikicchāya cittaṁ parisodheti.
root_text.an10.99:24.1: So ime pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe,
root_text.an10.99:24.2: vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.99:24.4: ‘nanvāyaṁ vihāro purimehi vihārehi abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’”ti?
root_text.an10.99:25.1: “Imampi kho, upāli, mama sāvakā attani dhammaṁ sampassamānā araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevanti, no ca kho tāva anuppattasadatthā viharanti.
root_text.an10.99:26.1: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, bhikkhu vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā …pe… dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.99:26.3: ‘nanvāyaṁ vihāro purimehi vihārehi abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’”ti?
root_text.an10.99:27.1: “Imampi kho, upāli, mama sāvakā attani dhammaṁ sampassamānā araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevanti, no ca kho tāva anuppattasadatthā viharanti.
root_text.an10.99:28.1: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, bhikkhu pītiyā ca virāgā …pe… tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.99:28.3: ‘nanvāyaṁ vihāro purimehi vihārehi abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’”ti?
root_text.an10.99:29.1: “Imampi kho, upāli, mama sāvakā attani dhammaṁ sampassamānā araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevanti, no ca kho tāva anuppattasadatthā viharanti.
root_text.an10.99:30.1: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, bhikkhu sukhassa ca pahānā …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ …pe….
root_text.an10.99:31.1: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, bhikkhu sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ‘ananto ākāso’ti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.99:31.3: ‘nanvāyaṁ vihāro purimehi vihārehi abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’”ti?
root_text.an10.99:32.1: “Imampi kho, upāli, mama sāvakā attani dhammaṁ sampassamānā araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevanti, no ca kho tāva anuppattasadatthā viharanti.
root_text.an10.99:33.1: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, bhikkhu sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘anantaṁ viññāṇan’ti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …pe….
root_text.an10.99:34.1: Sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘natthi kiñcī’ti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …pe….
root_text.an10.99:35.1: Sabbaso ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘santametaṁ paṇītametan’ti nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.99:35.3: ‘nanvāyaṁ vihāro purimehi vihārehi abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’”ti?
root_text.an10.99:36.1: “Imampi kho, upāli, mama sāvakā attani dhammaṁ sampassamānā araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevanti, no ca kho tāva anuppattasadatthā viharanti.
root_text.an10.99:37.1: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, bhikkhu sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati; paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti.
root_text.an10.99:37.3: ‘nanvāyaṁ vihāro purimehi vihārehi abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’”ti?
root_text.an10.99:38.1: “Imampi kho, upāli, mama sāvakā attani dhammaṁ sampassamānā araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevanti, anuppattasadatthā ca viharanti.

translation_text.an10.99:1.1: Then Venerable Upāli went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an10.99:1.2: “Sir, I wish to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.”
translation_text.an10.99:2.1: “Upāli, remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest are challenging.
translation_text.an10.99:2.2: It’s hard to maintain seclusion and hard to find joy in it.
translation_text.an10.99:2.3: Staying alone, the forests seem to rob the mind of a mendicant who isn’t immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.an10.99:2.5: ‘Though I don’t have immersion, I’m going to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.’ You can expect that
translation_text.an10.99:2.6: they’ll sink down or float away.
translation_text.an10.99:3.1: Suppose there was a large lake,
translation_text.an10.99:3.2: and along comes a bull elephant that’s three or three and a half meters tall.
translation_text.an10.99:3.4: ‘Why don’t I plunge into this lake and play around while washing my ears and back?
translation_text.an10.99:3.5: When I’ve bathed and drunk, I’ll emerge from the water and go wherever I want.’
translation_text.an10.99:3.6: And that’s just what he does.
translation_text.an10.99:3.9: Because his large incarnation finds a footing in the depths.
translation_text.an10.99:4.1: Then along comes a rabbit or a cat.
translation_text.an10.99:4.3: ‘What difference is there between me and a bull elephant?
translation_text.an10.99:4.4: Why don’t I plunge into this lake and play around while washing my ears and back?
translation_text.an10.99:4.5: When I’ve bathed and drunk, I’ll emerge from the water and go wherever I want.’
translation_text.an10.99:4.6: They jump into the lake rashly, without thinking.
translation_text.an10.99:4.8: they’ll sink down or float away.
translation_text.an10.99:4.10: Because their little incarnation finds no footing in the depths.
translation_text.an10.99:4.12: ‘Though I don’t have immersion, I’m going to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.’ You can expect that
translation_text.an10.99:4.13: they’ll sink down or float away.
translation_text.an10.99:5.1: Suppose there was a little baby boy playing in his own urine and feces.
translation_text.an10.99:5.3: Isn’t that a totally foolish game?”
translation_text.an10.99:6.1: “After some time that boy grows up and his faculties mature.
translation_text.an10.99:6.2: He accordingly plays childish games such as toy plows, tip-cat, somersaults, pinwheels, toy measures, toy carts, and toy bows.
translation_text.an10.99:6.4: Aren’t such games better than what he did before?”
translation_text.an10.99:7.1: “After some time that boy grows up and his faculties mature further.
translation_text.an10.99:7.2: He accordingly amuses himself, supplied and provided with the five kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.an10.99:7.3: Sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.an10.99:7.7: Touches known by the body, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.an10.99:7.9: Aren’t such games better than what he did before?”
translation_text.an10.99:8.1: “Now in this regard, Upāli, a Realized One arises in the world, perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.
translation_text.an10.99:8.2: He has realized with his own insight this world—with its gods, Māras, and divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, gods and humans—and he makes it known to others.
translation_text.an10.99:8.3: He proclaims a teaching that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And he reveals a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.an10.99:9.1: A householder hears that teaching, or a householder’s child, or someone reborn in a good family.
translation_text.an10.99:9.2: They gain faith in the Realized One
translation_text.an10.99:9.4: ‘Living in a house is cramped and dirty, but the life of one gone forth is wide open.
translation_text.an10.99:9.5: It’s not easy for someone living at home to lead the spiritual life utterly full and pure, like a polished shell.
translation_text.an10.99:9.6: Why don’t I shave off my hair and beard, dress in ocher robes, and go forth from the lay life to homelessness?’
translation_text.an10.99:10.1: After some time they give up a large or small fortune, and a large or small family circle. They shave off hair and beard, dress in ocher robes, and go forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.an10.99:11.1: Once they’ve gone forth, they take up the training and livelihood of the mendicants. They give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. They’re scrupulous and kind, living full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an10.99:12.1: They give up stealing. They take only what’s given, and expect only what’s given. They keep themselves clean by not thieving.
translation_text.an10.99:13.1: They give up unchastity. They are chaste, set apart, avoiding the vulgar act of sex.
translation_text.an10.99:14.1: They give up lying. They speak the truth and stick to the truth. They’re honest and dependable, and don’t trick the world with their words.
translation_text.an10.99:15.1: They give up divisive speech. They don’t repeat in one place what they heard in another so as to divide people against each other. Instead, they reconcile those who are divided and support those who are united, delighting in harmony, loving harmony, speaking words that promote harmony.
translation_text.an10.99:16.1: They give up harsh speech. They speak in a way that’s mellow, pleasing to the ear, lovely, going to the heart, polite, likable and agreeable to the people.
translation_text.an10.99:17.1: They give up talking nonsense. Their words are timely, true, and meaningful, in line with the teaching and training. They say things at the right time which are valuable, reasonable, succinct, and beneficial.
translation_text.an10.99:18.1: They refrain from injuring plants and seeds.
translation_text.an10.99:18.2: They eat in one part of the day, abstaining from eating at night and food at the wrong time.
translation_text.an10.99:18.3: They refrain from seeing shows of dancing, singing, and music .
translation_text.an10.99:18.4: They refrain from attiring and adorning themselves with garlands, fragrance, and makeup.
translation_text.an10.99:18.5: They refrain from high and luxurious beds.
translation_text.an10.99:18.6: They refrain from receiving gold and currency,
translation_text.an10.99:18.15: They refrain from running errands and messages;
translation_text.an10.99:18.19: mutilation, murder, abduction, banditry, plunder, and violence.
translation_text.an10.99:19.1: They’re content with robes to look after the body and almsfood to look after the belly.
translation_text.an10.99:19.2: Wherever they go, they set out taking only these things.
translation_text.an10.99:19.3: They’re like a bird: wherever it flies, wings are its only burden.
translation_text.an10.99:19.4: In the same way, a mendicant is content with robes to look after the body and almsfood to look after the belly. Wherever they go, they set out taking only these things.
translation_text.an10.99:19.5: When they have this entire spectrum of noble ethics, they experience a blameless happiness inside themselves.
translation_text.an10.99:20.1: When they see a sight with their eyes, they don’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an10.99:20.2: If the faculty of sight were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, they practice restraint, protecting the faculty of sight, and achieving restraint over it.
translation_text.an10.99:20.3: When they hear a sound with their ears …
translation_text.an10.99:20.4: When they smell an odor with their nose …
translation_text.an10.99:20.5: When they taste a flavor with their tongue …
translation_text.an10.99:20.6: When they feel a touch with their body …
translation_text.an10.99:20.7: When they know an idea with their mind, they don’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an10.99:20.8: If the faculty of mind were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, they practice restraint, protecting the faculty of mind, and achieving its restraint.
translation_text.an10.99:20.9: When they have this noble sense restraint, they experience an unsullied bliss inside themselves.
translation_text.an10.99:21.1: They act with situational awareness when going out and coming back; when looking ahead and aside; when bending and extending the limbs; when bearing the outer robe, bowl and robes; when eating, drinking, chewing, and tasting; when urinating and defecating; when walking, standing, sitting, sleeping, waking, speaking, and keeping silent.
translation_text.an10.99:22.1: When they have this entire spectrum of noble ethics, this noble sense restraint, and this noble mindfulness and situational awareness,
translation_text.an10.99:22.2: they frequent a secluded lodging—a wilderness, the root of a tree, a hill, a ravine, a mountain cave, a charnel ground, a forest, the open air, a heap of straw.
translation_text.an10.99:22.3: Gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, they sit down cross-legged, set their body straight, and establish mindfulness in their presence.
translation_text.an10.99:23.1: Giving up covetousness for the world, they meditate with a heart rid of covetousness, cleansing the mind of covetousness.
translation_text.an10.99:23.2: Giving up ill will and malevolence, they meditate with a mind rid of ill will, full of sympathy for all living beings, cleansing the mind of ill will.
translation_text.an10.99:23.3: Giving up dullness and drowsiness, they meditate with a mind rid of dullness and drowsiness, perceiving light, mindful and aware, cleansing the mind of dullness and drowsiness.
translation_text.an10.99:23.4: Giving up restlessness and remorse, they meditate without restlessness, their mind peaceful inside, cleansing the mind of restlessness and remorse.
translation_text.an10.99:23.5: Giving up doubt, they meditate having gone beyond doubt, not undecided about skillful qualities, cleansing the mind of doubt.
translation_text.an10.99:24.1: They give up these five hindrances, corruptions of the heart that weaken wisdom.
translation_text.an10.99:24.2: Then, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, they enter and remain in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an10.99:24.4: Isn’t this state better than what they had before?”
translation_text.an10.99:25.1: “When my disciples see this quality inside themselves they frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest. But so far they haven’t achieved their heart’s goal.
translation_text.an10.99:26.1: Furthermore, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, a mendicant enters and remains in the second absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of immersion, with internal clarity and mind at one, without placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an10.99:26.3: Isn’t this state better than what they had before?”
translation_text.an10.99:27.1: “When my disciples see this quality inside themselves they frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest. But so far they haven’t achieved their heart’s goal.
translation_text.an10.99:28.1: Furthermore, with the fading away of rapture, a mendicant enters and remains in the third absorption. They meditate with equanimity, mindful and aware, personally experiencing the bliss of which the noble ones declare, ‘Equanimous and mindful, one meditates in bliss.’
translation_text.an10.99:28.3: Isn’t this state better than what they had before?”
translation_text.an10.99:29.1: “When my disciples see this quality inside themselves they frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest. But so far they haven’t achieved their heart’s goal.
translation_text.an10.99:30.1: Furthermore, with the giving up of pleasure and pain and the disappearance of former happiness and sadness, a mendicant enters and remains in the fourth absorption, without pleasure or pain, with pure equanimity and mindfulness. …” …
translation_text.an10.99:31.1: “Furthermore, going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that ‘space is infinite’, a mendicant enters and remains in the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an10.99:31.3: Isn’t this state better than what they had before?”
translation_text.an10.99:32.1: “When my disciples see this quality inside themselves they frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest. But so far they haven’t achieved their heart’s goal.
translation_text.an10.99:33.1: Furthermore, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite space, aware that ‘consciousness is infinite’, a mendicant enters and remains in the dimension of infinite consciousness. …” …
translation_text.an10.99:34.1: “Going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, they enter and remain in the dimension of nothingness. …” …
translation_text.an10.99:35.1: “Going totally beyond the dimension of nothingness, aware that ‘this is peaceful, this is sublime’, they enter and remain in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an10.99:35.3: Isn’t this state better than what they had before?”
translation_text.an10.99:36.1: “When my disciples see this quality inside themselves they frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest. But so far they haven’t achieved their heart’s goal.
translation_text.an10.99:37.1: Furthermore, going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, they enter and remain in the cessation of perception and feeling. And, having seen with wisdom, their defilements come to an end.
translation_text.an10.99:37.3: Isn’t this state better than what they had before?”
translation_text.an10.99:38.1: “When my disciples see this quality inside themselves they frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest. And they have achieved their heart’s goal.
translation_text.an10.99:38.2: Come on, Upāli, stay with the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an10.99:38.3: If you stay with the Saṅgha you’ll be comfortable.”

variant_text.an10.99:2.1: Durabhisambhavāni hi kho → durabhisambhavāni kho (bj, pts1ed)
variant_text.an10.99:2.6: uplavissati vā’ti → uppilavissati vā (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
variant_text.an10.99:6.2: vaṅkakaṁ → vaṅkaṁ (si, pts1ed) | ciṅgulakaṁ → piṅgulikaṁ (sya-all); ciṅkulakaṁ (mr)
variant_text.an10.99:8.1: Idha kho pana vo → idaṁ padaṁ sya-all potthakesu natthi; voti nipātamattaṁ (aṭṭha.)
